metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4846
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 6: When activity-dependent TMR-dextran uptake was assessed in these neurons, mCer-Rab11Q70L (constitutively active form) significantly increased the number of nerve terminals performing activity-dependent bulk endocytosis (ADBE) (Fig. 6C). In contrast, all of the other Rab mutants had no effect (Fig. 6C).

Authors conclude; "We found that Rab11Q70L increased the number of nerve terminals performing ADBE. This effect was not due to increased SV exocytosis, since Rab11Q70L did not impact on this parameter. Rab11Q70L preferentially localizes to nerve terminals, as does endogenous Rab11 (SI Appendix, Fig. S13).". Experimental description: supplemental M&M: "Primary cultures of cerebellar granule neurons (CGNs) were prepared from the cerebella of 7 day old Sprague Dawley rat pups of both sexes as described"

to disambiguate "Rab11"; table 1 reports Rab11a (P62494), the streetname of Rab11a is Rab11
